entries.title: Department of Biosciences and Technology
entries.excerpt: The Department of Biosciences and Technology integrates molecular and cellular science, biotechnology, microbiology, bioinformatics, and translational research.
entries.text: Department of Biosciences and Technology Department of Biosciences and Technology The Department of Biosciences and Technology integrates molecular and cellular science, biotechnology, microbiology, bioinformatics, and translational research. School of Science & Environmental Studies Department of Biosciences and Technology Biological discovery translated into health, agriculture, and sustainable technology. 19 faculty 5 research themes 6 programmes Department Teaching grounded in active scientific work. The Department of Biosciences and Technology integrates molecular and cellular science, biotechnology, microbiology, bioinformatics, and translational research. People → Academic programmes → Research Areas of enquiry Research overview → 01 Cancer, cell & regenerative biology Cancer cell biology, genome stability, stem cells, tissue engineering, bone biology, and protein aggregation. 02 Genomics, bioinformatics & microbiomes Genomics, transcriptomics, metagenomics, microbial and human microbiomes, protein structure, and computational biology. 03 Microbiology, immunology & bioprocessing Applied and environmental microbiology, immune profiling, microbial metabolites, enzyme technology, and bioprocess development. 04 Plant molecular biology & sustainable biotechnology Plant development, defence and metabolism; plant–microbe interactions; genome editing; and crop improvement. 05 Biosensors, diagnostics & bioimaging Electrochemical and optical biosensors, pathogen and molecular diagnostics, bioimaging, and preclinical workflows. Academics Programmes All programmes → Undergraduate Integrated M.Sc. Biotechnology Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Postgraduate M.Sc. Biotechnology Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Postgraduate M.Sc. Microbiology Programme details ↗

entries.title: Department of Chemistry
entries.excerpt: The Department of Chemistry combines foundational chemical science and laboratory learning with faculty research in polymer science, synthesis and catalysis, functional and molecular materials, nanomaterials, environmental chemistry, and photonics.
entries.text: Department of Chemistry Department of Chemistry The Department of Chemistry combines foundational chemical science and laboratory learning with faculty research in polymer science, synthesis and catalysis, functional and molecular materials, nanomaterials, environmental chemistry, and photonics. School of Science & Environmental Studies Department of Chemistry Molecular science, functional materials, and chemistry for industry and society. 9 faculty 4 research themes 3 programmes Department Teaching grounded in active scientific work. The Department of Chemistry combines foundational chemical science and laboratory learning with faculty research in polymer science, synthesis and catalysis, functional and molecular materials, nanomaterials, environmental chemistry, and photonics. People → Academic programmes → Research Areas of enquiry Research overview → 01 Nanomaterials, molecular materials & photonics Nanomaterial synthesis, plasmonics and nanophotonics, metal-organic frameworks, molecular nanomagnets, switches, and fluorescent sensors. 02 Organic synthesis, catalysis & green chemistry Synthetic organic chemistry, ionic liquids, organocatalysis, heterogeneous catalysis, asymmetric synthesis, biocatalysis, and green synthesis. 03 Polymers, coatings & sustainable materials Liquid-crystalline and conducting polymers, biopolymers, nanocomposites, protective coatings, packaging, microplastics, and water purification. 04 Inorganic & environmental chemistry Metal complexes, wastewater treatment, pollutant degradation, and materials chemistry for environmental applications. Academics Programmes All programmes → Undergraduate B.Sc. Chemistry (Hons) Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Postgraduate M.Sc. Chemistry (Industrial Polymer Chemistry) Programme details ↗ (opens in

entries.title: Department of Environmental Studies
entries.excerpt: The department offers postgraduate and doctoral study spanning ecology and biodiversity, water and pollution, climate and sustainability, environmental assessment, and applied field research.
entries.text: Department of Environmental Studies Department of Environmental Studies The department offers postgraduate and doctoral study spanning ecology and biodiversity, water and pollution, climate and sustainability, environmental assessment, and applied field research. School of Science & Environmental Studies Department of Environmental Studies Environmental science, conservation, climate, and sustainability. 3 faculty 4 research themes 2 programmes Department Teaching grounded in active scientific work. The department offers postgraduate and doctoral study spanning ecology and biodiversity, water and pollution, climate and sustainability, environmental assessment, and applied field research. People → Academic programmes → Research Areas of enquiry Research overview → 01 Ecology, biodiversity & conservation Urban ecology, biogeography, species and habitat conservation, biodiversity informatics, and community-led restoration. 02 Water, pollution & environmental engineering River- and lake-bank filtration, emerging contaminants, water and wastewater treatment, and sustainable water systems. 03 Climate action & sustainability assessment Sustainable development goals, environmental impact assessment, life-cycle assessment, ESG, natural-resource management, and environmental innovation. 04 Citizen science & environmental communication Public participation, biodiversity data, environmental education, and science communication for conservation action. Academics Programmes All programmes → Postgraduate M.Sc. Environment Science Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Doctoral Ph.D. in Environmental Science Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) People One department, varied academic roles. entries.title: Department of Mathematics and Statistics
entries.excerpt: The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering.
entries.text: Department of Mathematics and Statistics Department of Mathematics and Statistics The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. School of Science & Environmental Studies Department of Mathematics and Statistics Structure, uncertainty, modelling, and data. 23 faculty 4 research themes 5 programmes Department Teaching grounded in active scientific work. The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. People → Academic programmes → Research Areas of enquiry Research overview → 01 Discrete mathematics & combinatorics Matroid and graph theory, algebra, lattice theory, combinatorics, coding theory, and discrete structures. 02 Analysis, modelling & differential equations Geometric function theory, differential equations, numerical analysis, elastodynamics, wave propagation, fluid mechanics, and mathematical modelling. 03 Statistics, data science & machine learning Sampling, ranked-set sampling, statistical inference, Bayesian methods, statistical genetics, time series, biostatistics, data science, and machine learning. 04 Optimisation & operations research Optimisation, decision-making, transportation and supply-chain models, and fuzzy and rough sets. Academics Programmes All programmes → Undergraduate B.Sc. Applied Statistics and Data Analytics (Hons) Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Postgraduate M.Sc. Mathematics and Data Science Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Postgraduate M.Sc. Applied Statistics Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Doctoral Ph.D. in Mathematics

entries.title: Department of Physics
entries.excerpt: The Department of Physics brings together teaching, laboratory learning, and research across fundamental, computational, optical, materials, and device physics.
entries.text: Department of Physics Department of Physics The Department of Physics brings together teaching, laboratory learning, and research across fundamental, computational, optical, materials, and device physics. School of Science & Environmental Studies Department of Physics From fundamental questions to photonic and quantum technologies. 14 faculty 4 research themes 3 programmes Department Teaching grounded in active scientific work. The Department of Physics brings together teaching, laboratory learning, and research across fundamental, computational, optical, materials, and device physics. People → Academic programmes → Research Areas of enquiry Research overview → 01 Quantum, optics & photonics Nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, nanophotonics, photonic systems, plasmonics, and spectroscopy. Photonics Research Group Quantum Science & Technology Group 02 Materials, devices & nanoscience Semiconductor and dielectric thin films, nanomaterials and nanocomposites, sensors, photodetectors, and functional devices. 03 Astrophysics & fundamental physics Cosmic magnetic fields, galactic dynamics, high-energy theory, gravitation, and the mathematical structure of fundamental interactions. 04 Applied & computational physics Soft matter, quantum chemistry and computational physics; radiation processing of polymers; liquid acoustics; dielectric spectroscopy; and corrosion science. Academics Programmes All programmes → Undergraduate B.Sc. Physics (Hons) Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Postgraduate M.Sc. Physics Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) Doctoral Ph.D. in Physics Programme details ↗ (opens in a new tab) People One department, varied academic roles. In December 2022, I proudly achieved a significant academic accomplishment by obtaining a Ph.D. in Statistics from the prestigious IIT (ISM) Dhanbad. I am now thrilled to be serving as an Assistant Professor at MIT WPU in Pune, where I can pursue my passion for teaching and research

entries.title: Dr. Soma Mishra
entries.excerpt: River- and lake-bank filtration; emerging contaminants; industrial and municipal wastewater treatment; bioaugmentation; phytoremediation; sustainable water and waste management.
entries.text: Dr. Soma Mishra Assistant Professor Environmental Studies River- and lake-bank filtration; emerging contaminants; industrial and municipal wastewater treatment; bioaugmentation; phytoremediation; sustainable water and waste management. Environmental engineer working on water systems, emerging contaminants, river- and lake-bank filtration, and sustainable wastewater treatment.

entries.title: Dr. Soumava Biswas
entries.excerpt: Metal-organic frameworks for gas storage and carbon capture, molecular nanomagnets, spin-crossover switches, and fluorescent sensors.
entries.text: Dr. Soumava Biswas Assistant Professor Chemistry Metal-organic frameworks for gas storage and carbon capture, molecular nanomagnets, spin-crossover switches, and fluorescent sensors. Dr. Soumava Biswas obtained his PhD in chemistry from IISER Bhopal, India under the supervision of Professor Sanjit Konar in 2017. Then he worked with the research group of Prof. Masahiro Yamashita, at Tohoku University, Japan as a JSPS postdoctoral fellow for two years

entries.title: Dr. Vaishali Hanumant Gaikwad
entries.excerpt: Doctoral research in inorganic chemistry focused on metal complexes and drug function.
entries.text: Dr. Vaishali Hanumant Gaikwad Assistant Professor Chemistry Doctoral research in inorganic chemistry focused on metal complexes and drug function. She has qualified CSIR -NET in 2007

entries.title: Facilities
entries.excerpt: Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Facilities · Department of Biosciences and Technology Facilities Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU. Biosciences & Technology Facilities Teaching and research environments across the department. 01 Revvity – MIT-WPU Centre of Excellence for Bioimaging Technologies and Innovation Confocal microscopy, flow cytometry, live-animal imaging, microfluidics, gene extraction, and advanced imaging software. 02 Cell culture and tissue engineering Animal-cell culture, tissue engineering, disease models, and regenerative-biology research. 03 Molecular biology, bioanalytics and bioinformatics Molecular and bioanalytical workflows, genomics, and computational bioscience. 04 Microbial and plant biotechnology Microbial culture, bioprocessing, plant biotechnology, and applied sustainable bioscience. Shared infrastructure MIT-WPU Research Facilities Significant shared research instruments and faculty contacts are maintained separately from routine teaching equipment. Central facilities

entries.title: Facilities
entries.excerpt: Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Facilities · Department of Chemistry Facilities Teaching and research facilities in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU. Chemistry Facilities Teaching and research environments across the department. 01 Chemical synthesis and analysis Laboratory teaching covers organic, inorganic, physical, and analytical chemistry, instrumental methods, and spectroscopic structure determination. 02 Polymer processing and characterisation Industrial Polymer Chemistry covers polymer synthesis, processing, raw-material analysis, coatings, testing, and characterisation. Shared infrastructure MIT-WPU Research Facilities Significant shared research instruments and faculty contacts are maintained separately from routine teaching equipment. Central facilities

entries.excerpt: Graph theory, discrete mathematics, and combinatorics.
entries.text: Mr. Amol Prakash Narke Assistant Professor Mathematics & Statistics Graph theory, discrete mathematics, and combinatorics. Mr. Amol Narke holds an M.Sc in Mathematics and has qualified the State Eligibility Test (SET) in Mathematics. With seven years of teaching experience, his research interests lie in Graph Theory and Matroid Theory

Research output Publications A selected bibliography across computation, optics, materials, and devices. Complete records remain available through individual academic profiles. This page presents selected work relevant to the group’s current scope; it is not a complete institutional bibliography.

entries.title: Quantum science across computation, light, and materials.
entries.excerpt: Quantum Science and Technology Group at MIT World Peace University, Pune.
entries.text: Quantum Science & Technology Group Quantum science across computation, light, and materials. Quantum Science and Technology Group at MIT World Peace University, Pune. Quantum research at MIT-WPU Quantum science across computation, light, and materials. An interdisciplinary group working across quantum information, algorithms, optics, materials, devices, and computing systems. Explore Read about the group’s research, people, selected publications, and developing academic programme. Research → Publications → About An interdisciplinary research group The group connects theoretical computer science and quantum information with experimental optics, materials science, photonic devices, and computing systems. Research Research directions Full research overview → Education Quantum Technologies An interdisciplinary Quantum Technologies course is in development. The approved syllabus and schedule will be published when available. Course information → Enquiries Research and collaboration Contact the group for research, academic, and institutional enquiries. Contact the group

entries.title: Quantum Technologies
entries.excerpt: Quantum Technologies education at MIT World Peace University.
entries.text: Education · Quantum Science & Technology Group Quantum Technologies Quantum Technologies education at MIT World Peace University. Education Quantum Technologies An interdisciplinary course is being prepared for the next academic semester. Course status In development The course will connect foundations of quantum science with computation, information, optics, materials, and devices. The approved syllabus, eligibility, credits, instructors, and timetable will be published after the university’s academic review is complete.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes of the Photonics Research Group at MIT World Peace University, Pune.
entries.text: Research · Photonics Research Group Research Research themes of the Photonics Research Group at MIT World Peace University, Pune. What we study Research The group’s current research spans structured and quantum light, nonlinear optical systems, and the characterization of photonic materials.

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes of the Quantum Science and Technology Group at MIT World Peace University.
entries.text: Research · Quantum Science & Technology Group Research Research themes of the Quantum Science and Technology Group at MIT World Peace University. entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Biosciences and Technology Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Biosciences and Technology at MIT-WPU. Biosciences & Technology Research The Department of Biosciences and Technology integrates molecular and cellular science, biotechnology, microbiology, bioinformatics, and translational research. 01 Cancer, cell & regenerative biology Cancer cell biology, genome stability, stem cells, tissue engineering, bone biology, and protein aggregation. 02 Genomics, bioinformatics & microbiomes Genomics, transcriptomics, metagenomics, microbial and human microbiomes, protein structure, and computational biology. 03 Microbiology, immunology & bioprocessing Applied and environmental microbiology, immune profiling, microbial metabolites, enzyme technology, and bioprocess development. 04 Plant molecular biology & sustainable biotechnology Plant development, defence and metabolism; plant–microbe interactions; genome editing; and crop improvement. 05 Biosensors, diagnostics & bioimaging Electrochemical and optical biosensors, pathogen and molecular diagnostics, bioimaging, and preclinical workflows. Work with us Research pathways Doctoral research Review the department’s doctoral programme, supervisors, and current research areas. Doctoral programmes → Student projects Students can identify relevant themes and contact faculty through the directory. Faculty profiles → Collaboration Use faculty profiles and research-group pages to find the closest scientific contact. Find a faculty member →

entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Chemistry Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Chemistry at MIT-WPU. Chemistry Research The Department of Chemistry combines foundational chemical science and laboratory learning with faculty research in polymer science, synthesis and catalysis, functional and molecular materials, nanomaterials, environmental chemistry, and photonics. 01 Nanomaterials, molecular materials & photonics Nanomaterial synthesis, plasmonics and nanophotonics, metal-organic frameworks, molecular nanomagnets, switches, and fluorescent sensors. 02 Organic synthesis, catalysis & green chemistry Synthetic organic chemistry, ionic liquids, organocatalysis, heterogeneous catalysis, asymmetric synthesis, biocatalysis, and green synthesis. 03 Polymers, coatings & sustainable materials Liquid-crystalline and conducting polymers, biopolymers, nanocomposites, protective coatings, packaging, microplastics, and water purification. 04 Inorganic & environmental chemistry Metal complexes, wastewater treatment, pollutant degradation, and materials chemistry for environmental applications. Work with us Research pathways Doctoral research Review the department’s doctoral programme, supervisors, and current research areas. entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Environmental Studies Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Environmental Studies at MIT-WPU. Environmental Studies Research The department offers postgraduate and doctoral study spanning ecology and biodiversity, water and pollution, climate and sustainability, environmental assessment, and applied field research. 01 Ecology, biodiversity & conservation Urban ecology, biogeography, species and habitat conservation, biodiversity informatics, and community-led restoration. 02 Water, pollution & environmental engineering River- and lake-bank filtration, emerging contaminants, water and wastewater treatment, and sustainable water systems. 03 Climate action & sustainability assessment Sustainable development goals, environmental impact assessment, life-cycle assessment, ESG, natural-resource management, and environmental innovation. 04 Citizen science & environmental communication Public participation, biodiversity data, environmental education, and science communication for conservation action. Work with us Research pathways Doctoral research Review the department’s doctoral programme, supervisors, and current research areas. entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Mathematics and Statistics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at MIT-WPU. Mathematics & Statistics Research The Department of Mathematics and Statistics connects rigorous mathematical foundations with statistics, computation, optimisation, data science, and applications across science and engineering. 01 Discrete mathematics & combinatorics Matroid and graph theory, algebra, lattice theory, combinatorics, coding theory, and discrete structures. 02 Analysis, modelling & differential equations Geometric function theory, differential equations, numerical analysis, elastodynamics, wave propagation, fluid mechanics, and mathematical modelling. 03 Statistics, data science & machine learning Sampling, ranked-set sampling, statistical inference, Bayesian methods, statistical genetics, time series, biostatistics, data science, and machine learning. 04 Optimisation & operations research Optimisation, decision-making, transportation and supply-chain models, and fuzzy and rough sets. Work with us Research pathways Doctoral research Review the department’s doctoral programme, supervisors, and current research areas. entries.title: Research
entries.excerpt: Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU.
entries.text: Research · Department of Physics Research Research themes, groups, and collaboration pathways in the Department of Physics at MIT-WPU. Physics Research The Department of Physics brings together teaching, laboratory learning, and research across fundamental, computational, optical, materials, and device physics. 01 Quantum, optics & photonics Nonlinear and quantum optics, structured light, nanophotonics, photonic systems, plasmonics, and spectroscopy. Photonics Research Group Quantum Science & Technology Group 02 Materials, devices & nanoscience Semiconductor and dielectric thin films, nanomaterials and nanocomposites, sensors, photodetectors, and functional devices. 03 Astrophysics & fundamental physics Cosmic magnetic fields, galactic dynamics, high-energy theory, gravitation, and the mathematical structure of fundamental interactions. 04 Applied & computational physics Soft matter, quantum chemistry and computational physics; radiation processing of polymers; liquid acoustics; dielectric spectroscopy; and corrosion science. entries.title: School of Science & Environmental Studies
entries.excerpt: Departments, research directions, people, programmes, and facilities in the MIT-WPU School of Science and Environmental Studies.
entries.text: School of Science & Environmental Studies School of Science & Environmental Studies Departments, research directions, people, programmes, and facilities in the MIT-WPU School of Science and Environmental Studies. MIT World Peace University School of Science & Environmental Studies Five departments connecting foundational education, laboratory practice, and research. Explore by department, research theme, academic programme, people, or event. Browse departments ↓ Departments Science at MIT-WPU 01 Physics From fundamental questions to photonic and quantum technologies. 14 faculty · 3 programmes · 4 research themes Open department → 02 Chemistry Molecular science, functional materials, and chemistry for industry and society. 9 faculty · 3 programmes · 4 research themes Open department → 03 Mathematics & Statistics Structure, uncertainty, modelling, and data. 23 faculty · 5 programmes · 4 research themes Open department → 04 Biosciences & Technology Biological discovery translated into health, agriculture, and sustainable technology. 19 faculty · 6 programmes · 5 research themes Open department → 05 Environmental Studies Environmental science, conservation, climate, and sustainability. 3 faculty · 2 programmes · 4 research themes Open department → Across departments Shared research directions Quantum, photonics & advanced materials Physics and Chemistry Data, modelling & computation Mathematics, Statistics, and all science departments Molecular life science & biotechnology Biosciences and Technology Environment, climate & sustainability Environmental Studies with cross-school collaboration Research groups Focused communities within the wider school. Research-group microsites carry detailed projects, publications, and member records without overloading department pages. Rese
